questions.question_text: Who creates the standard Notice of Agency Relationship form used in West Virginia?
distractors: Each brokerage creates their own form
The WV Bar Association drafts the form
The National Association of REALTORS provides it
questions.explanation: The WVREC promulgates a standard Notice of Agency Relationship form. This ensures uniform disclosure of agency relationships across all transactions in the state.

questions.question_text: What information does the WV Notice of Agency Relationship form identify?
questions.correct_answer: The type of representation the licensee provides
distractors: The commission percentage being charged
The licensee's years of experience
The broker's financial statement
questions.explanation: The Notice of Agency form identifies whether the licensee represents the seller, buyer, both (dual agent), landlord, tenant, or both landlord and tenant.

questions.question_text: In what format must WV agency relationships be disclosed?
questions.correct_answer: In writing using the Notice of Agency form
distractors: Verbally at the first meeting
Through an email within 48 hours
By posting it on the broker's website
questions.explanation: Every licensee must disclose in writing the agency relationship using the WVREC-promulgated Notice of Agency Relationship form. Verbal disclosure alone is insufficient.

questions.question_text: A salesperson begins working with a new buyer client. What form must be provided?
questions.correct_answer: The Notice of Agency Relationship form
distractors: The Property Condition Disclosure form
The Fair Housing Compliance form
The Trust Fund Authorization form
questions.explanation: The WV Notice of Agency Relationship form must be provided to disclose the agency relationship before the client signs any contract for representation.

questions.question_text: Can a WV licensee use a custom agency disclosure form instead of the WVREC form?
questions.correct_answer: No, the WVREC-promulgated form is required
distractors: Yes, any written disclosure is acceptable
Yes, with broker approval
Yes, if it contains the same information
questions.explanation: The WVREC promulgates the standard Notice of Agency Relationship form. Licensees must use this official form rather than custom alternatives to ensure consistency.

questions.question_text: When must a WV licensee provide the Notice of Agency Relationship form?
distractors: At the closing table during settlement
Within 72 hours of the first contact
After the first scheduled property showing
questions.explanation: The agency disclosure must be made before any person signs a contract for representation. This ensures the client understands the relationship before committing.

questions.question_text: A buyer signs a representation agreement without receiving the Notice of Agency form. What is the status of this situation?
questions.correct_answer: The licensee violated the disclosure requirement
distractors: The agreement is automatically void
No violation if disclosure is given at closing
The buyer can waive the disclosure requirement
questions.explanation: The licensee violated the requirement by failing to disclose the agency relationship before the contract was signed. Disclosure must occur before, not after, signing.

questions.question_text: The Notice of Agency form covers representation in which types of transactions?
distractors: Only residential sales transactions
Only commercial lease transactions
Only new construction purchases
questions.explanation: The form identifies representation for sellers, buyers, dual agency, landlords, tenants, or both landlord and tenant — covering both sales and rental transactions.

questions.question_text: A listing agent is working with an unrepresented buyer at an open house. Must the agent provide the Notice of Agency form?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, before the buyer signs any representation contract
distractors: No, open house visitors don't need agency disclosure
Only if the buyer makes an offer
Only if the buyer requests representation
questions.explanation: Every licensee must disclose agency relationships in writing before any person signs a contract for representation. If the buyer decides to enter a contract, disclosure must come first.

questions.question_text: A licensee changes their agency relationship mid-transaction from buyer's agent to dual agent. What is required?
questions.correct_answer: New written disclosure and both parties' consent
distractors: Verbal notification to both transaction parties
No additional disclosure or consent is needed
Only the buyer needs to provide written consent
questions.explanation: A change in agency relationship requires new written disclosure. Dual agency specifically requires informed written consent from both the buyer and seller.
